name: python3-dask
title: python3-dask
summary: Minimal task scheduling abstraction for Python 3
description:
Minimal task scheduling abstraction for Python 3
Dask is a flexible parallel computing library for analytics,
containing two components.
.
1. Dynamic task scheduling optimized for computation. This is similar
to Airflow, Luigi, Celery, or Make, but optimized for interactive
computational workloads.
2. "Big Data" collections like parallel arrays, dataframes, and lists
that extend common interfaces like NumPy, Pandas, or Python iterators
to larger-than-memory or distributed environments. These parallel
collections run on top of the dynamic task schedulers.
.
This contains the Python 3 version.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dask because Minimal task scheduling abstraction for Python 3.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dask as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dask [options] [args]` as documented upstream for dask.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dask with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dask --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
